content: We can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a computational implementation of the linguistic and pedagogical concept of reflection (reflexion) that is taught in high school literacy curricula, where structured metacognitive practice that improves student writing can be transferred to improve LLM self-reflection. Just as students are taught to revisit their own drafts to spot logical gaps and inconsistent arguments through guided reflexion, LLMs can leverage the same scaffolded iterative reflection routine to correct their own output errors.
